scope_note: This panel uses Yale's official first-year advice, requirements, and essay pages. Yale's guidance is school-wide and contextual rather than a rigid course-count formula.

areas.name: Overall rigor
areas.summary: Yale looks for students who have consistently taken a broad range of challenging courses and done well.
areas.detail: Yale says academic strength is its first consideration and that it looks for students who have consistently taken a broad range of challenging courses in high school and have done well, while evaluating each applicant in context.

areas.name: Context-based review
areas.summary: Yale says no student is disadvantaged for not taking advantage of a resource that was not available to them.
areas.detail: Yale explicitly says applicants are considered within their own context so that no student is disadvantaged for lacking a course or resource that was not available at their school.

areas.name: Recommendations
areas.summary: Yale requires letters of recommendation from two teachers and one counselor, plus a school report with transcript.
areas.detail: Yale's first-year requirements say the application should include letters of recommendation from two teachers and one counselor, along with the school report and transcript.

areas.name: Essays
areas.summary: Yale requires Yale-specific short-answer writing plus one essay response for Common App or Coalition applicants.
areas.detail: Yale's essay topics page says Common Application and Coalition applicants complete short-answer questions of about 35 words, additional Yale-specific responses including a 125-word reflection, and one 400-word essay prompt. QuestBridge applicants complete the corresponding Yale questionnaire in the status portal.
